role_title: Chief of Staff, VP of Product
secondary_archetype: Technical Program Manager with product leadership trajectory

seniority.level: Senior IC / Staff (likely equivalent to Staff PM or Senior Chief of Staff)
level_signals: 10 years of experience in product management, technical program management, strategy, or a role with real operational ownership
Comp range $211K-$312K base, consistent with senior IC at Scale AI
Expected to act on behalf of VP of Product and make real prioritization calls

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10 years of experience in product management, technical program management, strategy, or a role with real operational ownership - not coordination.
Demonstrated record of managing complexity: multiple parallel workstreams, competing stakeholder demands, and ambiguous ownership  and keeping all of it moving without dropping threads.
Extreme organizational precision. Their systems are legible to others, not just themselves.

Enough technical fluency to hold a real conversation with a platform engineer. Not deep coding, but genuine comprehension of how systems work: APIs, infrastructure, deployment, architecture at a conceptual level and curiosity to learn.
The judgment to act on behalf of the principal.
Strong, precise written communication. First drafts should be clean. Recaps, briefs, and async updates should require minimal editing.

scope.surface: Product organization at Scale AI; supporting VP of Product across all product workstreams
scope.business_model: B2B / B2G AI data and infrastructure platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Operational throughput of the product org
core_responsibilities: Prepare the VP for every significant meeting, external or internal: customer briefings, executive reviews, board-adjacent discussions with the right level of context and the right framing.
Represent the VP in internal workstreams where her presence isn't required but her perspective is.
Drive follow-through on decisions made in meetings: own the action items, chase closures, and surface blockers before they become problems.
Build and maintain the operational infrastructure that keeps the product org coordinated.
Engage directly with engineering leads, PMs, and go-to-market partners with enough technical and product fluency to move things forward.

compensation.equity_form: equity grant subject to Board of Director approval

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($211K-$312K base) and competitive
Clear and honest role framing — explicitly distinguishes this from a traditional CoS role
Explicit growth path described: 'wants to grow into product leadership'
Highly specific responsibilities signal genuine operational ownership rather than vague support
Scale AI works with high-profile enterprise and government clients (Meta, U.S. Army, etc.)
red_flags: 10-year minimum experience floor is high for a CoS role that is not explicitly a GM or executive title
Role requires being 'in every room, on every decision' — potential for scope creep and burnout
No remote option stated; onsite in SF, NY, or Seattle only
No explicit mention of visa sponsorship — unclear for international candidates
Wide comp band ($100K spread) may indicate significant uncertainty in leveling

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and 10-year floor)
scope.business_model (inferred from About Us section describing B2B/B2G AI data platform)
location.type (inferred as onsite from city-specific pay transparency disclosure and no remote language)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred false; no remote option mentioned)
ambiguities: Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; marked as 'unclear'.
Office attendance policy is not explicitly stated beyond the implication that this is an onsite role in SF, NY, or Seattle.
The wide compensation band ($211K-$312K) may reflect different leveling across locations or experience tiers, but this is not clarified.
